title: A Detailed look at Sacrifice, from Blackoath Entertainment (solo RPG)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FkK2Rp5xEIc

Daniel (@dungeondive) gives a thorough tour of Sacrifice, Alex T's "incense and iron" RPG from Blackoath Entertainment — a Berserk-and-Dark-Souls-flavoured, OSR-compatible D20 system. He compares the print-on-demand copies (his preferred play/scribble editions) against the LFOSR deluxe version, which ships with a textured cover, pamphlets, a map of Nea, and an actual stick of incense — beautiful, but bound too tightly to splay open at the table.

What makes Sacrifice stand out is its character creation. There are no classes; you begin at level three as a "branded" survivor — already a hardened warrior bearing a demonic mark that senses evil (and alerts evil to you), taking damage in its presence. You pick feats, proficiencies and a combat maneuver, then lean into systems Daniel loves: no magic at all, slot-based encumbrance, usage dice for ammo, stamina-fuelled combat maneuvers, a corruption track, and signature weapons "marked by darkness" that level up with new traits. His one real gripe: the hex-travel rules lack a terrain-generation chart, so solo players must import tools like Scarlet Heroes. He recommends pairing the base book with the Companion, which expands oracles, wounds and boss "complex duels" for solo play.

----

Do you prefer RPGs where your character starts already powerful and hard to kill, or do you want the fragile zero-to-hero climb?
